name: Productsup Product Content Syndication API
what_it_does: Productsup REST API for product content syndication and commerce experience management platform. Enables AI agents to manage product data ingestion from multiple sources (ERP, PIM, spreadsheets), handle data transformation and enrichment rule automation, access channel-specific product content export (Amazon, Walmart, Google Shopping), retrieve feed performance and error analytics, manage validation rules and data quality checks, handle category mapping and attribute transformation, access channel publishing and scheduling workflows, retrieve import and export job status, manage data source and channel connector configuration, and integrate product content with marketplace and advertising platforms.
use_cases: Managing product feed ingestion and transformation from catalog automation agents
Handling channel-specific content export from marketplace management agents
Retrieving feed performance and validation errors from data quality agents
Integrating product content with Amazon and Walmart from commerce automation agents
not_for: Full PIM without channel syndication and feed management focus
Single-channel sellers without multi-platform feed management needs
Small retailers without enterprise product content complexity
best_when: A mid-to-large retailer or brand using Productsup wants AI agents to automate product feed ingestion, content transformation, channel validation, marketplace publishing, and feed performance analytics.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Automated product feed publishing to marketplaces (Amazon, Walmart) can affect live listings — incorrect pricing or content publishes immediately to customers. Feed validation errors left uncorrected can suppress listings. Pricing automation must respect MAP policies.

auth.notes: Productsup uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. Account and site-level credentials with data source, channel, and analytics scopes. Developer documentation portal. Webhooks for import/export job and feed events. Connects to 2,500+ channels and marketplaces.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise content syndication subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-product and channel volume enterprise pricing; annual contracts
pricing.notes: Berlin, Germany. Founded 2010. Commerce experience management platform. $70M+ in funding. 900+ enterprise customers including IKEA, Sephora, and Beiersdorf. 2,500+ connected channels and marketplaces. Acquired by EQT (private equity) 2023. Strong in European retail. Competes with Salsify and Akeneo for product content syndication.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; bulk import/export operations async

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Automated channel publishing affects live marketplace listings — pricing and content errors go live immediately
Feed transformation logic is configured in Productsup UI — agents trigger jobs, not define transformation rules
No public MCP server — REST API with developer documentation portal
Import/export jobs are async — agent must poll job status after triggering, not expect synchronous results
EQT acquisition (2023) — verify API roadmap stability post-acquisition
Channel-specific validation rules vary significantly — validate feed against each channel's requirements before publishing
agent_readiness.security_notes: Product content and feed data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. EU/US data residency. Marketplace pricing and product listing sensitivity.

security_score_components.security_notes: Product feed and syndication data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. EU/US data residency. Marketplace listing and pricing sensitivity.
